master_row.title: Quantum Channels and Maps
master_row.canonical_statement: Quantum information evolves through channels; geometry influences how states are transmitted and degraded.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a series / summation relating ρ, k, K_k.
master_row.dictionary: A quantum channel (E) is a completely positive trace‑preserving (CPTP) map acting on density operators; in the Trawinistic framework, channels are parameterised by curvature and may include transport along geodesics and interaction with the TZP environment.
master_row.encyclopedia: A quantum channel is the admissible state-transition map for density operators, including TZP-curvature transport corrections. Interpretive role: This entry is the core transport object for the information branch. It supports measurement, noise, thermodynamic evolution, and communication primitives through a single channel language.
